parameters.query:
-- Step 1: Find exact duplicates by URL
SELECT 
  MIN(id) AS keep_id,
  MAX(id) AS remove_id,
  url,
  COUNT(*) AS duplicate_count
FROM properties
WHERE url IS NOT NULL AND url != ''
GROUP BY url
HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
LIMIT 100;

-- Step 2: Find near-duplicate properties (same location, price, bedrooms)
SELECT 
  p1.id AS id1,
  p2.id AS id2,
  p1.title AS title1,
  p2.title AS title2,
  p1.price,
  p1.bedrooms,
  p1.city,
  p1.province,
  p1.source AS source1,
  p2.source AS source2
FROM properties p1
JOIN properties p2 ON 
  p1.id < p2.id
  AND p1.price = p2.price
  AND p1.bedrooms = p2.bedrooms
  AND LOWER(p1.city) = LOWER(p2.city)
  AND p1.province = p2.province
  AND p1.status = 'active'
  AND p2.status = 'active'
  AND ABS(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (p1.created_at - p2.created_at))) < 86400
LIMIT 50;

-- Step 3: Find data quality issues
SELECT 
  'missing_price' AS issue_type,
  COUNT(*) AS count
FROM properties WHERE price = 0 OR price IS NULL AND status = 'active'
UNION ALL
SELECT 
  'missing_location' AS issue_type,
  COUNT(*) AS count
FROM properties WHERE (city IS NULL OR city = '') AND status = 'active'
UNION ALL
SELECT 
  'unrealistic_price_high' AS issue_type,
  COUNT(*) AS count
FROM properties WHERE price > 100000000 AND status = 'active'
UNION ALL
SELECT 
  'unrealistic_price_low' AS issue_type,
  COUNT(*) AS count
FROM properties WHERE price < 50000 AND price > 0 AND status = 'active'
UNION ALL
SELECT 
  'missing_property_type' AS issue_type,
  COUNT(*) AS count
FROM properties WHERE (property_type IS NULL OR property_type = 'unknown') AND status = 'active'
UNION ALL
SELECT 
  'stale_listings' AS issue_type,
  COUNT(*) AS count
FROM properties WHERE scraped_at < NOW() - INTERVAL '30 days' AND status = 'active';

-- Remove exact URL duplicates (keep the oldest record)
UPDATE properties
SET status = 'duplicate'
WHERE id IN (
  SELECT MAX(id)
  FROM properties
  WHERE url IS NOT NULL AND url != ''
  GROUP BY url
  HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
)
RETURNING id, listing_id, title;

-- Flag stale listings as inactive
UPDATE properties
SET status = 'stale'
WHERE scraped_at < NOW() - INTERVAL '30 days'
AND status = 'active'
RETURNING id, listing_id, title, scraped_at;
